Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util

Class FlixelSpriteUtil

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.FlixelSpriteUtil
public final class FlixelSpriteUtil extends Object
Helper class related to FlixelSprite.

These utilities are designed to work with FlixelGDX's normal Batch-based draw flow. Avoid using ShapeRenderer in core game rendering unless you control the render pipeline.

Do not call createWhitePixelTexture() every frame or per sprite instance in hot paths. That allocates new Pixmap and Texture objects and will spike heap usage on most JVMs. Prefer obtainWhitePixelTexture(FlixelAssetManager), which registers a single persistent texture with the asset manager (see FlixelDefaultAssetManager) and reuses it for the lifetime of the game.

fill(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelSprite, com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color) writes solid pixels into the active frame when possible, and setBrightness(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelSprite, float) adjusts tint like Animate brightness.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    WHITE_PIXEL_TEXTURE_KEY
    Fixed asset key for the framework-owned 1x1 white Texture registered via obtainWhitePixelTexture(FlixelAssetManager).

  • Field Details

    • WHITE_PIXEL_TEXTURE_KEY

      public static final String WHITE_PIXEL_TEXTURE_KEY
      Fixed asset key for the framework-owned 1x1 white Texture registered via obtainWhitePixelTexture(FlixelAssetManager).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • obtainWhitePixelTexture

      @NotNull public static @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture obtainWhitePixelTexture(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelAssetManager assets)
      Returns the shared 1x1 white Texture registered with assets. The first call creates the texture, wraps it in a FlixelGraphic with FlixelGraphic.setPersist(boolean) true, and registers it with FlixelAssetManager.registerWrapper(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.asset.FlixelPooledWrapper). Callers must not Texture.dispose() this texture; lifecycle follows the asset manager.
      Parameters:
      assets - Non-null manager from Flixel.ensureAssets().

    • createWhitePixelTexture

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture createWhitePixelTexture()
      Creates a 1x1 white pixel texture.

      Ownership: Prefer obtainWhitePixelTexture(FlixelAssetManager) so only one instance exists. If you call this directly, you own the returned texture and must dispose it.

      Returns:
      The created white pixel texture.

    • drawRect

      public static void drawRect(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture whitePixel, float x, float y, float w, float h, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
      Draws a rectangle with the given color.
      Parameters:
      batch - The batch to draw the rectangle on.
      whitePixel - The white pixel texture to use for the rectangle.
      x - The x position of the rectangle.
      y - The y position of the rectangle.
      w - The width of the rectangle.
      h - The height of the rectangle.
      color - The color of the rectangle.

    • drawBorder

      public static void drawBorder(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture whitePixel, float x, float y, float w, float h, float thickness, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
      Draws a border around the given rectangle.
      Parameters:
      batch - The batch to draw the border on.
      whitePixel - The white pixel texture to use for the border.
      x - The x position of the rectangle.
      y - The y position of the rectangle.
      w - The width of the rectangle.
      h - The height of the rectangle.
      thickness - The thickness of the border.
      color - The color of the border.

    • createLinearGradientPixmap

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Pixmap createLinearGradientPixmap(int width, int height, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color start, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color end, boolean horizontal)
      Creates a 2-color linear gradient Pixmap.

      Caller owns the returned Pixmap and must dispose of it through Pixmap.dispose() themselves.

      Parameters:
      width - The width of the gradient.
      height - The height of the gradient.
      start - The start color of the gradient.
      end - The end color of the gradient.
      horizontal - Whether the gradient is horizontal.
      Returns:
      The created gradient Pixmap.

    • createLinearGradientTexture

      public static com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture createLinearGradientTexture(int width, int height, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color start, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color end, boolean horizontal)
      Creates a 2-color linear gradient Texture.

      Caller owns the returned Texture and must dispose of it through Texture.dispose() themselves.

      Parameters:
      width - The width of the gradient.
      height - The height of the gradient.
      start - The start color of the gradient.
      end - The end color of the gradient.
      horizontal - Whether the gradient is horizontal.
      Returns:
      The created gradient Texture.

    • bound

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelSprite bound(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelSprite sprite, float minX, float maxX, float minY, float maxY)
      Clamps the sprite inside a world rectangle. When maxX or maxY are 0 or less, the game view size from Flixel.getViewWidth() / Flixel.getViewHeight() is used for that axis.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to bound.
      minX - The minimum x position.
      maxX - The maximum x position.
      minY - The minimum y position.
      maxY - The maximum y position.
      Returns:
      The bounded sprite.

    • screenWrap

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelSprite screenWrap(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelSprite sprite, boolean left, boolean right, boolean top, boolean bottom)
      Wraps the sprite when it leaves the game view (same default max as bound(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelSprite, float, float, float, float) when zeros).
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to wrap.
      left - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the left edge of the game view.
      right - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the right edge of the game view.
      top - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the top edge of the game view.
      bottom - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the bottom edge of the game view.
      Returns:
      The wrapped sprite.

    • cameraBound

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelSprite cameraBound(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelSprite sprite, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelCamera camera, boolean left, boolean right, boolean top, boolean bottom)
      Keeps the sprite inside the given camera world view (FlixelCamera.getViewLeft() etc.).
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to bound.
      camera - The camera to bound the sprite to.
      left - Whether to bound the sprite when it leaves the left edge of the camera view.
      right - Whether to bound the sprite when it leaves the right edge of the camera view.
      top - Whether to bound the sprite when it leaves the top edge of the camera view.
      bottom - Whether to bound the sprite when it leaves the bottom edge of the camera view.
      Returns:
      The bounded sprite.

    • cameraWrap

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelSprite cameraWrap(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelSprite sprite, @Nullable @Nullable FlixelCamera camera, boolean left, boolean right, boolean top, boolean bottom)
      Wraps the sprite relative to the camera view edges.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to wrap.
      camera - The camera to wrap the sprite relative to.
      left - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the left edge of the camera view.
      right - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the right edge of the camera view.
      top - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the top edge of the camera view.
      bottom - Whether to wrap the sprite when it leaves the bottom edge of the camera view.
      Returns:
      The wrapped sprite.

    • fill

      @NotNull public static @NotNull FlixelSprite fill(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelSprite sprite, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color fillColor)
      Fills the active bitmap area with a solid color, similar to HaxeFlixel FlxSpriteUtil.fill.

      When the sprite uses an owned pixmap-backed Texture (FlixelSprite.hasOwnedGraphic()), every pixel in the current TextureRegion (animation frame or static region) is overwritten via Texture.draw(Pixmap, int, int) without replacing the graphic.

      Otherwise (shared atlas, unloaded graphic, or non-pixmap GPU texture) this falls back to FlixelSprite.makeGraphic(int, int, Color) sized to the logical frame (FlixelFrame.originalWidth / FlixelFrame.originalHeight when set, else region size, else object size).

      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to fill.
      fillColor - The color to fill with (including alpha).
      Returns:
      sprite for chaining.

    • setBrightness

      public static void setBrightness(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelSprite sprite, float brightness)
      Adjusts RGB toward white (brightness = 1) or black (brightness = -1), matching Adobe Animate style brightness as described for FlixelGDX FlixelSpriteUtil.setBrightness. Alpha is preserved.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to set the brightness of.
      brightness - Clamped to [-1, 1].

    • drawLine

      public static void drawLine(@NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch batch, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Texture whitePixel, float startX, float startY, float endX, float endY, float thickness, @NotNull @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.Color color)
      Draws a line segment using a 1x1 texture and Batch. Does not allocate.
      Parameters:
      batch - The batch to draw the line on.
      whitePixel - The white pixel texture to use for the line.
      startX - The x position of the start of the line.
      startY - The y position of the start of the line.
      endX - The x position of the end of the line.
      endY - The y position of the end of the line.
      thickness - The thickness of the line.
      color - The color of the line.

    • getMidpoint

      @NotNull public static @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 getMidpoint(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelSprite sprite, @Nullable @Nullable com.badlogic.gdx.math.Vector2 out)
      Midpoint of the sprite AABB in world space. Reuses out when non-null.
      Parameters:
      sprite - The sprite to get the midpoint of.
      out - The vector to store the midpoint in.
      Returns:
      The midpoint of the sprite.
